briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, highlighting risks in embedded finance infrastructure.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network in 2026, signalling a major shift away from US-dominated card networks.

- **[Would you give an AI agent your credit card? Companies are betting so](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/ai-shopping-agentic-payments-9.7222389?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Companies are racing to develop agentic payment systems that allow AI assistants to autonomously make purchases on behalf of consumers.

- **[Kalshi to make some users reveal job details to tackle insider trading](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2vn7wp33po?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Prediction market platform Kalshi is introducing new disclosure rules requiring certain users to share their employment details to combat insider trading.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada keeps key interest rate at 2.25%](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/interest-rate-bank-of-canada-june-2026-9.7229759?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada held its benchmark interest rate steady at 2.25% on June 10, as policymakers monitor economic conditions amid ongoing trade uncertainty.

- **[Coinbase Canada appoints Eric Richmond as new CEO](https://betakit.com/coinbase-canada-appoints-eric-richmond-as-new-ceo/)** — *BetaKit* — Coinbase Canada has named Eric Richmond as its new chief executive, signalling continued investment in the Canadian crypto and digital asset market.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada formally flagged screen scraping as a security risk and outlined its ambitions for a regulated open banking framework — a policy journey that continues to shape today's Canadian fintech landscape.

- **[OpenAI files for IPO as AI firms look to make Wall Street debut](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/open-ai-ipo-wall-street-9.7228209?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — OpenAI has filed for an IPO as a wave of AI companies look to access public capital markets, with implications for Canadian AI and fintech investment activity.

- **[CUSMA a blessing for some Canadian businesses, a pain for others despite escaping U.S. tariffs](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/cusma-costs-small-business-9.7222827?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian small businesses are navigating mixed outcomes under CUSMA, with compliance costs creating friction even for those avoiding direct U.S. tariff exposure.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking frameworks reshape payments competition, directly relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs preparing for Canada's open banking rollout.

- **[Edmonton Unlimited showcase broadens investment horizons for deep tech founders](https://betakit.com/edmonton-unlimited-showcase-broadens-investment-horizons-for-deep-tech-founders/)** — *BetaKit* — Edmonton's ecosystem-building model for connecting deep tech founders with investors offers a replicable blueprint for Atlantic Canadian fintech ecosystem development efforts.

- **[Why cybersecurity teams are struggling to keep up with AI adoption](https://betakit.com/why-cybersecurity-teams-are-struggling-to-keep-up-with-ai-adoption/)** — *BetaKit* — As Atlantic Canadian financial institutions accelerate AI adoption, this piece highlights the growing cybersecurity gap that fintechs and credit unions must proactively address.
